title: Praxis School Librarian (5312) - 200 Source Questions + 300 Guided Review
description: Contains 200 source diagnostic questions and 300 source-derived guided-review tasks; the assistant audit found 26 distinct source content kernels. Two 100-item diagnostics and a 120-item simulation with source-rich feedback and learning support for beginning school librarians.
contentReview: Assistant audit completed: the 200 source questions contain 26 distinct content kernels and 174 parallel variants under the normalized answer-set/rationale/reference test. The 300 additional activities are assistant-reviewed guided reasoning transformations, not independent exam questions. The 500-distinct-question target is not met.
simulationNote: The simulation matches the published 5312 question count, time, and five-domain distribution. It uses single-choice practice items; the official test may use several selected-response interaction types.
disclaimer: Independent preparation material, not affiliated with or endorsed by ETS, AASL, or ALA. Results are not official forms, scaled scores, pass predictions, licenses, legal advice, copyright determinations, privacy determinations, or substitutes for current state and local requirements.

domains.label: Information Access in the Learning Environment

items.prompt: A school librarian is reviewing needs assessment after noticing uneven learner outcomes. Which action is most defensible?
choices: Combine disaggregated use, achievement, curriculum, stakeholder, and access evidence before setting measurable program priorities.
Let circulation totals alone determine every priority.
Copy last year’s plan without reviewing current needs.
Survey only frequent library users and generalize to everyone.
items.rationale: A defensible needs assessment triangulates quantitative and qualitative evidence and looks for groups whose needs or access may be hidden by schoolwide averages.
choiceRationales: Correct. A defensible needs assessment triangulates quantitative and qualitative evidence and looks for groups whose needs or access may be hidden by schoolwide averages.

Not the best answer. "Let circulation totals alone determine every priority." substitutes a narrow, unsupported, inequitable, or procedurally weak action for the evidence-based approach. A defensible needs assessment triangulates quantitative and qualitative evidence and looks for groups whose needs or access may be hidden by schoolwide averages.

Not the best answer. "Copy last year’s plan without reviewing current needs." substitutes a narrow, unsupported, inequitable, or procedurally weak action for the evidence-based approach. A defensible needs assessment triangulates quantitative and qualitative evidence and looks for groups whose needs or access may be hidden by schoolwide averages.

Not the best answer. "Survey only frequent library users and generalize to everyone." substitutes a narrow, unsupported, inequitable, or procedurally weak action for the evidence-based approach. A defensible needs assessment triangulates quantitative and qualitative evidence and looks for groups whose needs or access may be hidden by schoolwide averages.

items.prompt: A school librarian is reviewing mission and goals after noticing uneven learner outcomes. Which action is most defensible?
choices: Align the library mission and measurable goals with school improvement priorities, learner needs, intellectual freedom, and equitable access.
Write goals around equipment purchases rather than learner outcomes.
Adopt a generic mission without stakeholder input.
Treat the library as separate from curriculum and school improvement.
items.rationale: Program goals should connect library services to learning while preserving the school librarian’s professional responsibilities and the rights of all learners.
choiceRationales: Correct. Program goals should connect library services to learning while preserving the school librarian’s professional responsibilities and the rights of all learners.

Not the best answer. "Write goals around equipment purchases rather than learner outcomes." substitutes a narrow, unsupported, inequitable, or procedurally weak action for the evidence-based approach. Program goals should connect library services to learning while preserving the school librarian’s professional responsibilities and the rights of all learners.

Not the best answer. "Treat the library as separate from curriculum and school improvement." substitutes a narrow, unsupported, inequitable, or procedurally weak action for the evidence-based approach. Program goals should connect library services to learning while preserving the school librarian’s professional responsibilities and the rights of all learners.

items.prompt: A school librarian is reviewing budget stewardship after noticing uneven learner outcomes. Which action is most defensible?
choices: Build a transparent, evidence-based budget that connects expenditures to priorities, lifecycle costs, accessibility, and equitable learner benefit.
Spend the remaining budget on the newest products regardless of need.
Divide funds equally across formats without examining use or access.
Exclude renewal, maintenance, licensing, and replacement costs.
items.rationale: Sound budgeting considers total cost of ownership, documented needs, instructional value, accessibility, and whether resources expand equitable opportunity.
choiceRationales: Correct. Sound budgeting considers total cost of ownership, documented needs, instructional value, accessibility, and whether resources expand equitable opportunity.

Not the best answer. "Spend the remaining budget on the newest products regardless of need." substitutes a narrow, unsupported, inequitable, or procedurally weak action for the evidence-based approach. Sound budgeting considers total cost of ownership, documented needs, instructional value, accessibility, and whether resources expand equitable opportunity.

Not the best answer. "Divide funds equally across formats without examining use or access." substitutes a narrow, unsupported, inequitable, or procedurally weak action for the evidence-based approach. Sound budgeting considers total cost of ownership, documented needs, instructional value, accessibility, and whether resources expand equitable opportunity.

Not the best answer. "Exclude renewal, maintenance, licensing, and replacement costs." substitutes a narrow, unsupported, inequitable, or procedurally weak action for the evidence-based approach. Sound budgeting considers total cost of ownership, documented needs, instructional value, accessibility, and whether resources expand equitable opportunity.

items.prompt: A school librarian is reviewing program evaluation after noticing uneven learner outcomes. Which action is most defensible?
choices: Use process, participation, access, and learner-outcome evidence to evaluate implementation and revise the program.
Report only how many books were checked out.
Change several variables without documenting implementation.
Claim impact from satisfaction comments alone.
items.rationale: Evaluation is strongest when measures match stated goals, implementation is documented, evidence is disaggregated, and findings lead to a monitored improvement cycle.
choiceRationales: Correct. Evaluation is strongest when measures match stated goals, implementation is documented, evidence is disaggregated, and findings lead to a monitored improvement cycle.

Not the best answer. "Report only how many books were checked out." substitutes a narrow, unsupported, inequitable, or procedurally weak action for the evidence-based approach. Evaluation is strongest when measures match stated goals, implementation is documented, evidence is disaggregated, and findings lead to a monitored improvement cycle.

Not the best answer. "Change several variables without documenting implementation." substitutes a narrow, unsupported, inequitable, or procedurally weak action for the evidence-based approach. Evaluation is strongest when measures match stated goals, implementation is documented, evidence is disaggregated, and findings lead to a monitored improvement cycle.

Evaluation is strongest when measures match stated goals, implementation is documented, evidence is disaggregated, and findings lead to a monitored improvement cycle.

items.prompt: A school librarian is reviewing policy and operations after noticing uneven learner outcomes. Which action is most defensible?
choices: Develop clear, consistently applied procedures with stakeholder input, current law and policy, safety, privacy, reconsideration, and appeal pathways.
Resolve each concern informally without written criteria.
Apply different access rules to favored viewpoints.
Promise absolute confidentiality regardless of safety or legal duties.
items.rationale: Written, viewpoint-neutral procedures protect access and due process while helping staff respond consistently and consult authorized decision makers when facts are complex.
choiceRationales: Correct. Written, viewpoint-neutral procedures protect access and due process while helping staff respond consistently and consult authorized decision makers when facts are complex.

Not the best answer. "Resolve each concern informally without written criteria." substitutes a narrow, unsupported, inequitable, or procedurally weak action for the evidence-based approach. Written, viewpoint-neutral procedures protect access and due process while helping staff respond consistently and consult authorized decision makers when facts are complex.

Not the best answer. "Apply different access rules to favored viewpoints." substitutes a narrow, unsupported, inequitable, or procedurally weak action for the evidence-based approach. Written, viewpoint-neutral procedures protect access and due process while helping staff respond consistently and consult authorized decision makers when facts are complex.

Not the best answer. "Promise absolute confidentiality regardless of safety or legal duties." substitutes a narrow, unsupported, inequitable, or procedurally weak action for the evidence-based approach. Written, viewpoint-neutral procedures protect access and due process while helping staff respond consistently and consult authorized decision makers when facts are complex.

items.prompt: A school librarian is reviewing collection development after noticing uneven learner outcomes. Which action is most defensible?
choices: Use a board-approved selection policy, curriculum and community evidence, professional reviews, diversity audits, and format/accessibility needs.
Select only titles personally preferred by the librarian.
Use popularity as the sole selection criterion.
Avoid resources that present unfamiliar perspectives.
items.rationale: Collection decisions should follow documented criteria and build a relevant, representative, accessible collection rather than reflecting one person’s preferences.
choiceRationales: Correct. Collection decisions should follow documented criteria and build a relevant, representative, accessible collection rather than reflecting one person’s preferences.

Not the best answer. "Select only titles personally preferred by the librarian." substitutes a narrow, unsupported, inequitable, or procedurally weak action for the evidence-based approach. items.prompt: A school librarian is reviewing equitable access and scheduling after noticing uneven learner outcomes. Which action is most defensible?

items.prompt: A school librarian is reviewing circulation privacy and resource sharing after noticing uneven learner outcomes. Which action is most defensible?

items.prompt: A school librarian is reviewing intellectual freedom and challenges after noticing uneven learner outcomes. Which action is most defensible?

items.prompt: A school librarian is reviewing copyright and licensing after noticing uneven learner outcomes. Which action is most defensible?

items.rationale: Attribution and copyright compliance are related but distinct; educators examine purpose, nature, amount, market effect, license terms, and current institutional guidance.

Correct. Attribution and copyright compliance are related but distinct; educators examine purpose, nature, amount, market effect, license terms, and current institutional guidance.

items.prompt: A school librarian is reviewing digital citizenship privacy and security after noticing uneven learner outcomes. Which action is most defensible?
choices: Reduce digital citizenship to a one-time password lecture.
Require public accounts without reviewing age and privacy terms.
Collect sensitive student data because a free tool requests it.
Teach learners to manage identity, consent, data sharing, security, respectful participation, algorithms, and platform risks through authentic decisions.
items.rationale: Digital citizenship combines critical participation with privacy and security; tools should be vetted for instructional need, accessibility, data practices, and age-appropriate use.

items.prompt: A school librarian is reviewing learning objectives and alignment after noticing uneven learner outcomes. Which action is most defensible?

items.prompt: A school librarian is reviewing formative assessment and feedback after noticing uneven learner outcomes. Which action is most defensible?

items.prompt: A school librarian is reviewing learning environment and management after noticing uneven learner outcomes. Which action is most defensible?

items.prompt: In a parallel school, during collaborative planning, a team disagrees about learning environment and management. What should the school librarian recommend first?
choices: Demand silence everywhere regardless of the learning activity.

Establish co-created routines, zones, transitions, technology expectations, restorative responses, and flexible spaces that support simultaneous learning needs.
items.rationale: A well-managed library makes expectations teachable and predictable while using space, supervision, relationships, and proportionate responses to preserve access and safety.

items.prompt: In a parallel school, during collaborative planning, a team disagrees about technology integration. What should the school librarian recommend first?

items.prompt: In a parallel school, during collaborative planning, a team disagrees about leadership and partnerships. What should the school librarian recommend first?

assistantReview.limitation: Normalized answer-set, rationale, and reference reuse is counted as one content kernel. Source-derived transformations are approved as guided practice, not as independent exam questions. Assistant review is not independent licensed-professional review or psychometric validation.

bankDisclosure: This pack has 500 learning activities, not 500 independent exam questions: 200 source questions (26 distinct content kernels; 174 parallel variants) plus 300 source-derived guided-review activities. 474 newly authored independent questions are needed to reach the 500-question target.
